Free Text: Dated 22/06/17. Japanese Knotweed is growing to the left of the farm crossing gate on NR land. It has begun to migrate through the post & wire fence onto the adjacent farmland. There are several small patches approx. 1m. in height across an area of approx. 20sq.m. With the current weather & high temperature it is expected to grow rapidly in the near future. This is an established patch; as there is evidence of large cane section <dead>, under nearby redundant railway materials.

Great Crested Newts breed in ponds adjacent to the railway line & anywhere within the mileage, lineside shown is potential habitat. Great Crested Newts & the places they shelter are protected by law. As at Feb 2004, a DEFRA licence to undertake work in the area is being prepared. Until this is granted, only essential emergency work is allowed within the mileage shown. In the interim, temporary Great Crested Newt procedures & a newt information handout have been prepared for staff & contractors undertaking such work. It is essential procedures are adhered to as failure to comply may result in English Nature taking company/individual to court. Temporary procedures & guidance notes are available from: Chis Davis, Regional Environmental Manager LNE; Chris.davis@networkrail.co.uk, tel: 01904 522944.

Local Name: Birchwood Lane to Blackwell South Junction Approach
Free Text:
Access is gained via Birchwood Lane, Somercotes.

Access through the double gates at the bottom of the slope.  Once through the gates turn right and drive on the access road alongside the Up Erewash, through the disused Alfreton Old Tunnel and up towards the approach of Blackwell South Junction as required. 

Pedestrian access can also be gained by parking in the North of Alfreton Station car park compound on the Down Erewash side.  Walk to the station platform and use the pedestrian footbridge to cross over to the Up Erewash side.

Free Text: ZIAR Ref No_281816_ Interim Reference No_HZ_27550 _Notes: Waiting shelters both down side and up-side in poor condiiotn.
